title: Spectral Consistency Distillation: Frequency-Aware Few-Step Generation for Flow Matching Models
description: We propose Spectral Consistency Distillation (SCD), a frequency-aware distillation framework that decomposes the consistency distillation loss into spectral frequency bands and applies per-band adaptive weighting and teacher supervision. By upweighting high-frequency components and using more teacher ODE steps for high-frequency supervision targets, SCD preserves fine-grained textures and details that standard distillation methods lose, achieving higher-quality few-step generation without additional inference cost.
motivation: Flow matching and diffusion models produce high-quality samples but require many ODE steps (20-100 NFEs). Consistency distillation enables 1-4 step generation but degrades high-frequency details (textures, edges) because the standard MSE distillation loss treats all spatial frequencies equally. Since low-frequency components converge much faster than high-frequency ones along the ODE trajectory, uniform treatment wastes model capacity on already-converged low frequencies while under-investing in harder high-frequency components. No existing distillation method addresses this spectral imbalance.
proposed_approach: SCD modifies consistency distillation in three ways: (1) Spectral Loss Decomposition — the distillation loss is computed in the DCT domain with per-band weights that emphasize high frequencies; (2) Frequency-Adaptive Teacher Supervision — different frequency bands receive teacher targets computed with different numbers of ODE steps (more steps for high-frequency bands that need higher-quality targets); (3) Progressive Spectral Refinement — a training curriculum that first establishes basic generation capability with uniform weights, then progressively shifts emphasis to high-frequency fidelity. At inference time, the student model operates identically to a standard consistency model with zero additional overhead.

related_work.title: Flow Matching for Generative Modeling
related_work.authors: Yaron Lipman, Ricky T. Q. Chen, Heli Ben-Hamu, Maximilian Nickel, Matt Le
related_work.relevance: Foundation paper for flow matching — our teacher model backbone. SCD distills pretrained flow matching models.

related_work.title: Consistency Models
related_work.authors: Yang Song, Prafulla Dhariwal, Mark Chen, Ilya Sutskever
related_work.relevance: Introduced consistency distillation for few-step generation. SCD modifies the consistency distillation loss with spectral decomposition.

related_work.title: Improved Techniques for Training Consistency Models
related_work.relevance: Improved consistency training with Pseudo-Huber losses and lognormal schedules. SCD addresses an orthogonal axis (spectral loss) and can be combined with these improvements.

related_work.title: Consistency Models Made Easy
related_work.authors: Zhengyang Geng, Ashwini Pokle, William Luo, Justin Lin, J. Zico Kolter
related_work.relevance: Showed consistency tuning from pretrained models is efficient. SCD follows this paradigm — distilling from a pretrained teacher.

related_work.title: Consistency Flow Matching: Defining Straight Flows with Velocity Consistency
related_work.authors: Ling Yang, Zixiang Zhang, Zhilong Zhang, Xingchao Liu, Minkai Xu, Wentao Zhang, Chenlin Meng, Stefano Ermon, Bin Cui
related_work.relevance: Enforces velocity consistency in flow matching for straight flows. Complementary to SCD — they address trajectory straightness, we address spectral fidelity.

related_work.title: Progressive Distillation for Fast Sampling of Diffusion Models
related_work.relevance: Iterative step-halving distillation baseline. SCD is compared against this approach.

related_work.title: Flow Straight and Fast: Learning to Generate and Transfer Data with Rectified Flow
related_work.authors: Xingchao Liu, Chengyue Gong, Qiang Liu
related_work.relevance: Rectified flow learns straight ODE paths for few-step generation. Used as a baseline and potential teacher model.

related_work.title: Spectral Regularization for Diffusion Models
related_work.authors: Satish Chandran, Nicolas Roque dos Santos, Yunshu Wu, Greg Ver Steeg, Evangelos Papalexakis
related_work.relevance: Adds spectral losses during training (not distillation). SCD applies spectral decomposition to the distillation objective — a different setting with different challenges.

related_work.title: Guidance in the Frequency Domain Enables High-Fidelity Sampling at Low CFG Scales
related_work.authors: Seyedmorteza Sadat, Tobias Vontobel, Farnood Salehi, Romann M. Weber
related_work.relevance: Applies frequency-domain analysis to inference-time guidance. SCD applies frequency analysis to training-time distillation — complementary contributions.

related_work.title: Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers
related_work.relevance: Introduced DiT architecture. We use DiT-based models as one of our experimental backbones.

hypothesis: Decomposing the consistency distillation loss into frequency bands and applying higher weights and more teacher ODE steps to high-frequency components will reduce FID by 10-25% compared to standard consistency distillation at the same number of inference steps (1, 2, 4), with the improvement primarily driven by reduced high-frequency error.
success_criteria: SCD consistently outperforms standard consistency distillation in FID across CIFAR-10 and ImageNet-64 at all step budgets (1, 2, 4 steps)
Per-frequency error analysis shows SCD specifically reduces high-frequency error (top 50% DCT bands) by at least 15% compared to baseline consistency distillation
Visual inspection confirms improved texture and edge quality in SCD-generated images
Training overhead of SCD is less than 50% above baseline consistency distillation
